The Science

What Are Peptides?

Peptides are short chains of amino acids — the same building blocks that make up proteins in your body. Think of them as tiny messengers that tell your cells what to do: repair, regenerate, burn fat, glow.

Unlike synthetic chemicals, peptides work with your body’s natural systems. They signal your cells to produce more collagen, release growth hormone, burn stored fat, or fight inflammation — naturally and precisely.

Read the Science Guide

“The science around peptides has completely transformed how we think about weight management, longevity and cellular health.”

— Longevity Medicine Review & NEJM 2023

“The science around peptides and GLP-1 medications has completely transformed how we think about weight management, longevity and cellular health.”

— Longevity Medicine Review & NEJM 2023

⚕ Important: Murilé BioScience strongly recommends working with a qualified healthcare professional when beginning any peptide protocol.
01

Peptide Enters the Body

Administered subcutaneously, the peptide is absorbed rapidly into the bloodstream and travels to target tissues.

02

Binds to Receptors

Like a key into a lock, each peptide binds to specific receptors on your cells, triggering precise biological responses.

03

Signals Your Cells

Your cells receive the signal — burn fat, produce collagen, repair tissue, or boost immunity — and respond accordingly.

04

You See Results

Over weeks, visible improvements emerge: better body composition, glowing skin, more energy, faster recovery.

Why spend endlessly on beauty products when you can invest in your natural beauty from the inside out?

A Different Approach

Natural Beauty Starts Within

The average person spends thousands each year on topical skincare products that work only on the surface. Peptide wellness works at the cellular level — stimulating collagen from within, brightening through antioxidant pathways, and restoring what your skin naturally lost with age.

One consistent peptide protocol can replace a shelf full of expensive creams — with results that are deeper, longer-lasting, and biologically driven rather than surface-coated.
